title: Discovery Service API: Application and Service Registration (Service Registry Core)
description: Implement the CRUD API endpoints for registering and managing applications and services in the Discovery Service. Applications are top-level organizational units; services represent deployable components within an app. Services have a (service_id, env) unique identity and can be associated with producer/consumer sentinels.
objectives: Deterministic register/update/deactivate API for services with strict validation
Optional blockchain integration: publish service binding to ServiceRegistry on-chain

steps: Step 1 - Apps Endpoints: POST /api/v1/apps (create), GET /api/v1/apps (list, paginated), GET /api/v1/apps/{app_id}, PATCH /api/v1/apps/{app_id} (update display_name/owner), DELETE /api/v1/apps/{app_id} → deactivate (soft delete). Requires operator role.

Step 2 - Services Endpoints: POST /api/v1/services (create), GET /api/v1/services (list with filters: env, app_id, is_active), GET /api/v1/services/{service_id_or_uuid}, PATCH /api/v1/services/{id} (update display_name, description only), POST /api/v1/services/{id}/deactivate. Requires operator role.

Step 3 - Request/Response Models (Pydantic v2): CreateServiceRequest: app_id (UUID), service_id (str, max 64 chars, regex ^[a-z0-9-_]+$), env (Literal['dev','test','prod']), display_name (str), description (str, optional), owner_did (str, optional). ServiceResponse: all fields plus id, created_at, sentinel_count.

Step 4 - service_id Validation: service_id MUST match ^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9\-_]{1,62}[a-z0-9]$ (lowercase alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores; no leading/trailing hyphens). service_id is immutable after creation — PATCH cannot modify it. Return 409 if already exists.
Step 5 - Pagination: All list endpoints accept cursor (base64 encoded last ID) and limit (max 100, default 20). Response includes next_cursor and total_count.

Step 6 - Blockchain Integration (optional, BLOCKCHAIN_INTEGRATION=true): After service creation, call ServiceRegistryClient.register_service(). Store tx_receipt in services table (tx_hash column). If chain fails: log warning, mark chain_sync_pending=true. A background retry worker picks up pending services.

Step 7 - Deactivation Logic: POST /api/v1/services/{id}/deactivate: (a) set is_active=false, (b) trigger sentinel decommission for all associated active sentinels (emit internal event), (c) write audit event. Requires security-admin role (not just operator).

Step 8 - Input Sanitization: All string fields sanitized: strip surrounding whitespace, reject null bytes, URL fields validated with Pydantic AnyHttpUrl. description max 2000 chars. No HTML allowed in any text field.

error_codes.description: Attempt to change service_id via PATCH
service_id does not match allowed pattern

security_constraints: service_id regex validation prevents injection characters
owner_did field not validated for DID format at this layer — validated during onboarding
URL fields in metadata validated with Pydantic to prevent SSRF payload storage
Deactivation requires security-admin role — operator cannot deactivate

acceptance_criteria: POST /api/v1/services creates service, returns 201 with service object
Audit event written for every POST/PATCH/deactivate
